sections.paragraph:
- Original: Λούκιος

- Transliteration: Loukios

- Phonetic: loo'-kee-os

- Definition: Lucius = "light: bright: white"

1. a man from Cyrene who was a prophet and a teacher of the church in Antioch (Acts 13:1)

- Origin: of Latin origin

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Of Latin origin; illuminative; Lucius a Christian: - Lucius.
